title: Informal Settlement Health Access Gaps In Low- And Middle-Income Country Cities
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring health-service access gaps in informal settlements of LMIC cities, linking settlement-boundary maps, population data, and health-facility data at neighborhood scale.
why_it_matters: Over 1 billion people live in informal settlements, a number projected to reach 3 billion by 2050. Informal-settlement residents face compounded health risks — overcrowding, poor water and sanitation, and lack of health facilities — but are systematically invisible in official health statistics. Satellite-derived settlement maps now enable neighborhood-scale analysis, but linking settlement boundaries to health-facility access has not been done systematically.

success_metrics.target: Every access-gap estimate links settlement maps, population data, and facility data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
